ai.name: AI & Tech
ai.desc: Models · agent tooling · research · industry moves

stories.head: TSMC posts record NT$27.25 EPS — yet chip stocks sell off on AI-spending doubts
stories.brief: Record TSMC quarter fails to quell AI-spending doubts; chip selloff drives Taiwan correction
stories.tldr: TSMC reported second-quarter EPS of NT$27.25 [1], but record earnings from the world's largest chipmaker failed to quell doubts over the sustainability of AI spending, dragging markets down in a global chip-stock selloff [2]. The rout then deepened as the TSMC selloff drove a correction across Taiwan's semiconductor sector [3].
stories.why: As a semiconductor and data-center investor, record earnings triggering a selloff is the signal that the AI trade is now priced on perfection — watch for repricing across your US and Bursa chip exposure.

sources.excerpt: TSMC Reports Second Quarter EPS of NT$27.25|Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company Limited
a global selloff in chip stocks dragged markets down Thursday, as record earnings from TSMC, the world's largest chipmaker, failed to quell doubts over the sustainability of AI spending
Chip Stock Rout Deepens as TSMC Selloff Drives Taiwan Correction

stories.head: Thinking Machines releases Inkling — first ~1T-param open model with native audio/image
stories.brief: Inkling: a ~1T-param, 1M-context open model natively taking text, image and audio
stories.tldr: Thinking Machines released Inkling, described as the first large open model with roughly 1 trillion parameters and a 1 million context window to natively receive image, text and audio inputs, trained on 45 trillion tokens of text, images, audio and video [1].
stories.why: As an engineer building AI agents, a ~1T-param open model with a 1M context window and native audio/image intake is a potential open alternative to hosted APIs for multimodal agent work.

sources.excerpt: the first large open model with ~1T parameters and 1M context window to natively receive image, text, and audio inputs, trained on 45 trillion tokens of text, images, audio and video.

stories.head: Google ships Gemini 3.6 Flash — stronger agentic work, fewer tokens, lower price
stories.brief: Gemini 3.6 Flash lifts agentic and multimodal work while cutting token use and price
stories.tldr: Google released Gemini 3.6 Flash, offering stronger performance on complex agentic and multimodal tasks while reducing token usage, at a lower price point than 3.5 Flash [1].
stories.why: Building agents on Claude, a cheaper Gemini Flash with stronger agentic and multimodal performance is a competitive datapoint and a candidate model for cost-sensitive agent routing.

sources.excerpt: Gemini 3.6 Flash: "Stronger performance on complex agentic and multimodal tasks while reducing token usage. [...] at a lower price point than 3.5 Flash."

stories.head: Anthropic open-sources Claude for Teachers K-12 skills, built with Learning Commons
stories.brief: Open-source K-12 teaching skills give Claude academic standards across all 50 US states
stories.tldr: Anthropic open-sourced the source code and evaluation framework for the agent skills included with Claude for Teachers, co-developed with Learning Commons to help Claude create classroom materials grounded in academic standards and learning-science best practices [1]. The connector gives Claude access to academic standards across all 50 states [1].
stories.why: Even though it targets US K-12, the open-sourced skill code shows how Anthropic structures grounded, standards-aware agent skills — a useful reference architecture for the Claude agents you build.

sources.excerpt: Source code and evaluation framework for the agent skills that are included with Claude for Teachers. [...] co-developed between Anthropic and Learning Commons, who collaborated to help Claude create classroom materials that are grounded in academic standards and follow best practices from learning science research. [...] This connector provides Claude with access to academic standards across all 50 states and progressions beneath them.

stories.head: Malaysia's H2 2026 outlook: steady rates, semiconductor and data centre to drive growth
stories.brief: Steady rates seen anchoring Malaysia's H2, with chips and data centres leading growth
stories.tldr: Malaysia's H2 2026 outlook is framed around steady interest rates, with the semiconductor and data-centre sectors expected to drive growth [1].
stories.why: As a Bursa investor tracking the semiconductor and data-centre theme, this sets the macro backdrop for your Malaysian holdings — steady rates plus those two sectors as the named growth drivers into year-end.

sources.excerpt: Malaysia's 2H2026 Outlook: Steady Rates, Semiconductor And Data Centre To Drive Growth

stories.head: Epic's Lore VCS v0.8.5 adds low-level C API revision-tree read verbs
stories.brief: Lore VCS v0.8.5 implements C API verbs for reading the revision tree
stories.tldr: Epic Games' open-source Lore VCS shipped v0.8.5, implementing the low-level revision-tree read verbs on its C API — tree_load/tree_close, resolve_path, list_children, revision and node info, and node_path [1].
stories.why: For your UE5 pipeline work, Epic maturing the C API of its open-source version-control system is worth tracking if you're weighing tooling for large game projects with binary-heavy assets.

sources.excerpt: Implement the low-level revision-tree read verbs on the C API: `tree_load`/`tree_close`, `resolve_path`, `list_children`, revision & node `info`, and `node_path`

stories.head: Epic's Lore VCS gains service-process support on Linux and macOS
stories.brief: New Lore commit brings the version-control service process to Linux and macOS
stories.tldr: A commit to Epic Games' Lore VCS by Mattias Jansson adds support for running the Lore service process on Linux and macOS [1].
stories.why: Extending Lore's service process beyond Windows widens where it can run — relevant if your UE5 build and tooling setup spans Linux or macOS machines.

sources.excerpt: lore: Support the service process on Linux and macOS

Personal study compilation (CC BY-NC 4.0) by an anonymous GitHub user; explicitly secondary; no editorial oversight or institutional accountability; original Moonshot sources not captured.

Bitget is a cryptocurrency exchange; its news desk is content aggregation with no original financial journalism credentials or editorial standing — insufficient authority for semiconductor equity earnings reporting.

Excerpt is solely the generic page title with zero guidance figures; the specific 40% full-year growth guidance and $44.6–$45.8B Q3 guidance are entirely absent from the excerpt — substantial extrapolation beyond what the excerpt supports.
ts2.tech is a satellite technology company blog, not a credible financial news source; no authority for Philadelphia Semiconductor Index data; article date estimated rather than confirmed.

Generic evergreen tracker article title with no specific figures; all key FOMC claim details (July 28-29 dates, 3.50-3.75% rate, member counts) absent from excerpt; URL has no date component — specific claim cannot be attributed to this excerpt.

Financial opinion/analysis site; key claim data ($64.1B annual revenue, $26.5B Nasdaq listing) absent from excerpt; 'Must-Own AI Stock?' framing confirms opinion rather than primary financial reporting.
